news.headline: Nektar (NKTR) Launches Global Trials for Novel Atopic Dermatitis Treatment
news.summary: Nektar officially began its five-trial Phase 3 ZENITH-AD program by enrolling the first two global registrational studies, ZENITH AD-1 and ZENITH AD-2, in biologic- and JAK inhibitor-naive moderate-to-severe atopic dermatitis patients aged 12 and up. The launch marks the transition of rezpegaldesleukin from proof-of-concept data into pivotal testing.

news.headline: Nektar Therapeutics Announces Start of Phase 3 ZENITH AD Program Evaluating Novel Regulatory T-Cell Biologic Rezpegaldesleukin in Moderate-to-Severe Atopic Dermatitis
news.summary: Nektar's own release confirms ZENITH AD-1 and ZENITH AD-2 will each enroll about 510 treatment-naive patients, with a third trial (ZENITH AD-3) in biologic/JAK-experienced patients slated to start in September 2026. The trials were designed with FDA and EMA input, and initial Phase 3 topline data are still expected in mid-2028, with a planned BLA submission in 2029.

news.headline: Nektar Therapeutics (NKTR) Stock Looks Above Fair Value Despite Its 7x Run
news.summary: A July 20, 2026 valuation review found NKTR trading at roughly 41.5x price-to-sales versus a pharmaceuticals-industry average near 6.1x and a peer average around 11.2x, well above Simply Wall St's estimated fair multiple of about 17.5x. The piece concludes that after the stock's roughly 7x three-year run, a lot of optimism about rezpegaldesleukin's future revenue is already priced in, leaving little room for disappointment.

news.headline: Nektar Therapeutics (NASDAQ:NKTR) Stock Price Up 5.2% - What's Next?
news.summary: NKTR shares gained about 5% on July 9, 2026, extending the summer rally amid continued sector-wide optimism in immunology/dermatology biotech; the stock carries a consensus 'Strong Buy'-leaning rating from analysts (roughly seven to ten Buy, one Hold) with price targets spanning from Wedbush's $80 to Piper Sandler's $192 and an average consensus around $153.

news.headline: Nektar Therapeutics Stock Clocks Best Day In Over 2 Months As Investors Await Upcoming Clinical Data In Eczema
news.summary: NKTR shares jumped roughly 10.65% after Canaccord flagged Nektar as a likely sentiment beneficiary of AbbVie's planned $10.9 billion acquisition of Apogee Therapeutics in immunology/dermatology, with investors also positioning ahead of the July 2026 start of the Phase 3 ZENITH-AD program.

news.headline: How Nektar Therapeutics' Broad FTSE Russell Index Inclusion Will Impact NKTR Investors
news.summary: Nektar was added to the Russell 2000, 2500 and 3000 indices (and related growth/value/small-cap variants) in the June 2026 FTSE Russell reconstitution, a move expected to draw passive, benchmark-tracking fund flows into the stock.

catalysts.label: Phase 3 ZENITH AD-3 initiation (biologic/JAK-experienced patients)
catalysts.detail: Following the July 21, 2026 start of ZENITH AD-1 and AD-2 (each enrolling ~510 biologic- and JAK inhibitor-naive atopic dermatitis patients), Nektar plans to begin ZENITH AD-3, a third registrational trial of similar size enrolling patients with prior systemic biologic and/or JAK inhibitor treatment experience, in September 2026.

catalysts.label: Q2 2026 earnings report
catalysts.detail: Third-party earnings calendars (e.g. MarketBeat, StockAnalysis, TipRanks) list Nektar's next quarterly results date as August 6, 2026, covering the quarter ended June 30, 2026; the report is expected to include commentary on ZENITH-AD enrollment progress and cash runway (currently guided into 2028). Not yet formally confirmed via a Nektar press release as of July 22, 2026.

catalysts.label: Phase 3 alopecia areata program start
catalysts.detail: Nektar plans to advance rezpegaldesleukin into Phase 3 testing for severe-to-very-severe alopecia areata, building on positive Phase 2b REZOLVE-AA proof-of-concept and 52-week extension data (25.8% and 27.6% SALT<=20 response at week 52 for low- and high-dose arms versus 6.7% placebo), and the drug's FDA Fast Track designation.

catalysts.label: New-indication proof-of-concept study start
catalysts.detail: Nektar intends to initiate a proof-of-concept study of rezpegaldesleukin in at least one additional autoimmune/inflammatory indication beyond AD and alopecia areata (candidates discussed include vitiligo, lupus and psoriasis), with initial data expected in 2027.

catalysts.label: First ZENITH-AD Phase 3 data readout
catalysts.detail: Topline results from the ZENITH AD-1 and AD-2 biologic-naive atopic dermatitis studies are expected, marking the first pivotal efficacy readout for rezpegaldesleukin.

catalysts.label: Planned BLA submission
catalysts.detail: Nektar has guided to a Biologics License Application submission for rezpegaldesleukin in atopic dermatitis in 2029, contingent on positive Phase 3 results.

pulse: As of July 22, 2026, Nektar's story remains centered on the July 21 launch of its pivotal Phase 3 ZENITH-AD program, which dosed the first patients in the ZENITH AD-1 and AD-2 trials, with a third trial (AD-3) due to start in September 2026. Shares have rallied strongly since mid-June, helped by the program launch, a June FTSE Russell index-inclusion flow boost, and spillover optimism from AbbVie's pending $10.9 billion acquisition of Apogee Therapeutics in immunology/dermatology, though a July 20 Simply Wall St valuation piece cautioned that NKTR's roughly 41.5x price-to-sales multiple (versus an industry average near 6x) already prices in a great deal of optimism about rezpegaldesleukin, whose first pivotal AD data won't read out until mid-2028. Analysts remain broadly bullish (consensus 'Strong Buy', price targets spanning Wedbush's $80 to Piper Sandler's $192, averaging around $153), with the next scheduled catalyst being Q2 2026 earnings, unofficially calendared for August 6, 2026.
